//comments-1: TypeScript config used ONLY for type-checking — no compilation, no emit. The codebase stays .js / .jsx; we layer types via JSDoc + ambient .d.ts files so adoption is incremental and there are no .ts -> .js rewrites to coordinate.
//comments-2: checkJs:true makes tsc analyse .js files when they carry `// @ts-check` at the top (or via the per-file pragma). Files WITHOUT @ts-check are ignored. This is how we opt files in one at a time without breaking the build the moment a single .js file has a type issue.
//comments-3: include is intentionally narrow — type-checking the entire codebase day one would surface 1000s of errors and bury the signal. Each new file in a future PR adds itself here when it adopts @ts-check.
